description: Investment philosophy and process to discover strong investment ideas before the competition. Strives to achieve maximum investment returns at minimum capital risk while promoting advancement.

# Who we are
The firm’s culture is built around independent thinkers with a relentless drive to excel: investment professionals who were born and raised in the developing world and who bring firsthand expertise to each region they cover. The team’s deep cultural fluency and on-the-ground presence form a structural research advantage that cannot be replicated.
At CHANGE Global, our people are self-made immigrants who have personally witnessed the economic transformation in emerging markets. This firsthand perspective is the foundation of our investment edge.
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/69a5f55b8659f024d9e96d3d/1773866620022-46EIAI1856SNGXG4L9WW/unsplash-image-GkacI-_mGlg.jpg)
Meet the Team
* ## Thea Jamison, CFA
FOUNDER AND MANAGING DIRECTOR
Ms. Jamison possesses more than 20 years of investing experience and as one of the earliest investors focused on emerging and frontier markets, has been at the forefront of bringing U.S. institutional investment to the developing world for more than two decades. Over the course of her career, she has researched opportunities in over 90 countries and invested directly on 60 local stock exchanges. Ms. Jamison is widely recognized as a thought leader and is a frequent speaker at influential industry conferences and a contributor to major global financial media outlets. She began her career with Allianz Global Investors (previously Nicholas Applegate Capital Management) before founding CHANGE Global. Ms. Jamison earned her Master of Business Administration degree from Stanford University. She is a Chartered Financial Analyst and a member of the CFA Society of New York.
* ## Cristina Garcia
HEAD OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT, MD
Cristina brings over fifteen years of institutional investment experience, including a decade at BlackRock, where she partnered with global institutional investors across public and private markets through a wide range of markets and mandates. Over the course of her career, Ms. Garcia has worked with sovereign wealth funds, public pension plans, multilateral institutions, and global asset managers, with a focus on aligning investment solutions to client objectives and long-term outcomes. Previously, Ms. Garcia worked at Jefferies covering central banks and sovereign wealth funds. Ms. Garcia earned her Master of Business Administration degree from NYU Stern and serves as Chair of the Education Committee for 100 Women in Finance in New York.
* ## Brian K. Mugabe
SENIOR ANALYST
Mr. Mugabe is responsible for company and industry research in the region of Africa and the Middle East. He has twenty-five years of industry experience, with a focus on fundamental research and investment analysis. Prior to joining CHANGE Global Investment, he held positions as Head of Africa Research at Momentum (Imara S.P. Reid), Securities Africa, and Imara Edwards. Mr. Mugabe obtained his Bachelor of Commerce from Rhodes University in South Africa.
* ## Ken W. Liu, CFA
SENIOR ANALYST
Mr. Liu is responsible for company and industry research in the region of Asia. He has twenty years of industry experience, with a focus on fundamental research and investment analysis. Prior to joining the firm, he held Equity Analyst positions at KGI Asia, Yuanta Securities, and Pacific Star Partners. Mr. Liu was a Silicon Valley engineer before entering the financial services industry. Mr. Liu obtained his Master of Business Administration from the University of California. He is a Chartered Financial Analyst and a member of the CFA Society Orange County.
* ## Renata Abdurakhmanova Sand, CFA
SENIOR ANALYST
Ms. Sand is responsible for company and industry research in the region of Central and Eastern Europe. She has fifteen years of industry experience, focusing on fundamental research and investment analysis. Previously, she worked as a Research Associate at Frontier Market Asset Management and as a Credit Analyst at Kazkommertsbank in Kazakhstan. Ms. Sand obtained her Master of Business Administration degree from California State University. She is a Chartered Financial Analyst and a member of the CFA Society Spain.
* ## Khan Anastasiuo Vu
RESEARCH ANALYST
Ms. Vu is responsible for company and industry research in the region of Asia. She has ten years of investment experience, including four years under mentorship at CHANGE Global Investment. Prior to joining the firm, Ms. Vu held positions as Portfolio Analyst and Quantitative Analyst at Aspen Capital, a specialty real estate investment company. Ms. Vu earned her Master of Science in Financial Analysis degree from Portland State University and her Master of Business Administration degree from Concordia University. Ms. Vu is a Level II candidate for the Chartered Financial Analyst designation.
* ## Brad Power
ESG ANALYST
Bradley Power joined CHANGE Global Investment in 2019 and serves a dual role on the investment team, contributing to fundamental equity research while also leading the firm's proprietary ESG research effort. His sustainability analysis spans the full portfolio, integrating environmental, social, and governance considerations into the investment process through the firm's internally developed scoring framework.
* ## Nicole Kelleher
CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER
Ms. Kelleher oversees the general administration and day to day operational aspects of the company. She has twenty-five years of industry experience in investment operations. Prior to joining CHANGE Global Investment, she spent ten years as a senior manager at State Street where she served as Assistant Vice President of the Alternative Investment Solutions unit, with responsibilities for fund accounting, new products, and overseas expansion. Prior to State Street, Ms. Kelleher was an Associate Director, managing the institutional equity, fixed income, and alternative investment operations teams at Babson Capital Management for seven years. Ms. Kelleher attended the School of Arts and Science at the University of Massachusetts.
* ## Michael Wood
INVESTMENT OPERATIONS AND TRADING
Michael brings institutional investment operations and trading experience to CHANGE Global. He previously served as a Senior Associate in Investment Administration at Cohen & Steers Capital Management, overseeing trade settlement, portfolio monitoring, and reconciliation for an $80 billion manager, and helping lead the firm's T+1 settlement transition. Earlier, he was an Advanced Specialist on Bloomberg's AIM Trade Desk, advising institutional clients on trade lifecycle, portfolio management, and compliance workflows. He holds dual B.S. degrees in Finance and Accounting from Syracuse University's Whitman School of Management and is a CFA Level II candidate.”
* ## Kristin Travis
INVESTOR RELATIONS ANALYST
Ms. Travis is responsible for handling investor relations. As such, she works side by side with the business team in investor outreach and client servicing. Ms. Travis has ten years of industry experience. Previously, she held the position of a Financial Analyst and a Contract Administrator at Loral, and worked as a Senior Financial Analyst at Stanford Telecom. Ms. Travis obtained her Master of Business Administration from Santa Clara University.
